libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: For tests that create or resolve JNDI References for JavaBeans, set system property `com.mchange.v2.naming.referenceableJavaBeanClassWhitelist` to the exact fully qualified test bean class name before calling `JavaBeanReferenceMaker.createReference(...)` or `JavaBeanObjectFactory.getObjectInstance(...)`, and restore/clear it afterward. If using `ReferenceableUtils.referenceToObject(...)` overloads without an explicit allowed factory set, also set `com.mchange.v2.naming.objectFactoryWhitelist` to the exact test ObjectFactory class name; prefer explicit allow sets when possible. Do not use `*` unless the test is deliberately covering the unsafe opt-out behavior.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8716 [Automation] java run fails for com.mchange:mchange-commons-java:0.6.0; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The whitelist properties are global system properties; leaking them across tests can hide failures or produce intersection behavior if another config source also sets a whitelist.
Generated inner test classes used as whitelisted JavaBeans or ObjectFactories still need normal reachability coverage in native-image tests.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: mchange-commons-java 0.6.0 enforces JNDI JavaBean/reference security whitelists; tests that exercise JavaBeanReferenceMaker or JavaBeanObjectFactory will fail until the test bean class is explicitly whitelisted.
